1676	fn handle_estop_command(
1677	    config: &Config,
1678	    estop_command: Option<EstopSubcommands>,
1679	    level: Option<EstopLevelArg>,
1680	    domains: Vec<String>,
1681	    tools: Vec<String>,
1682	) -> Result<()> {
1683	    if !config.security.estop.enabled {
1684	        bail!("Emergency stop is disabled. Enable [security.estop].enabled = true in config.toml");
1685	    }
1686	
1687	    let config_dir = config
1688	        .config_path
1689	        .parent()
1690	        .context("Config path must have a parent directory")?;
1691	    let mut manager = security::EstopManager::load(&config.security.estop, config_dir)?;
1692	
1693	    match estop_command {
1694	        Some(EstopSubcommands::Status) => {
1695	            print_estop_status(&manager.status());
1696	            Ok(())
1697	        }
1698	        Some(EstopSubcommands::Resume {
1699	            network,
1700	            domains,
1701	            tools,
1702	            otp,
1703	        }) => {
1704	            let selector = build_resume_selector(network, domains, tools)?;
1705	            let mut otp_code = otp;
1706	            let otp_validator = if config.security.estop.require_otp_to_resume {
1707	                if !config.security.otp.enabled {
1708	                    bail!(
1709	                        "security.estop.require_otp_to_resume=true but security.otp.enabled=false"
1710	                    );
1711	                }
1712	                if otp_code.is_none() {
1713	                    let entered = Password::new()
1714	                        .with_prompt("Enter OTP code")
1715	                        .allow_empty_password(false)
1716	                        .interact()?;
1717	                    otp_code = Some(entered);
1718	                }
1719	
1720	                let store = security::SecretStore::new(config_dir, config.secrets.encrypt);
1721	                let (validator, enrollment_uri) =
1722	                    security::OtpValidator::from_config(&config.security.otp, config_dir, &store)?;
1723	                if let Some(uri) = enrollment_uri {
1724	                    println!("Initialized OTP secret for ZeroClaw.");
1725	                    println!("Enrollment URI: {uri}");
1726	                }
1727	                Some(validator)
1728	            } else {
1729	                None
1730	            };
1731	
1732	            manager.resume(selector, otp_code.as_deref(), otp_validator.as_ref())?;
1733	            println!("Estop resume completed.");
1734	            print_estop_status(&manager.status());
1735	            Ok(())
1736	        }
1737	        None => {
1738	            let engage_level = build_engage_level(level, domains, tools)?;
1739	            manager.engage(engage_level)?;
1740	            println!("Estop engaged.");
1741	            print_estop_status(&manager.status());
1742	            Ok(())
1743	        }
1744	    }
1745	}

1747	fn build_engage_level(
1748	    level: Option<EstopLevelArg>,
1749	    domains: Vec<String>,
1750	    tools: Vec<String>,
1751	) -> Result<security::EstopLevel> {
1752	    let requested = level.unwrap_or(EstopLevelArg::KillAll);
1753	    match requested {
1754	        EstopLevelArg::KillAll => {
1755	            if !domains.is_empty() || !tools.is_empty() {
1756	                bail!("--domain/--tool are only valid with --level domain-block/tool-freeze");
1757	            }
1758	            Ok(security::EstopLevel::KillAll)
1759	        }
1760	        EstopLevelArg::NetworkKill => {
1761	            if !domains.is_empty() || !tools.is_empty() {
1762	                bail!("--domain/--tool are not valid with --level network-kill");
1763	            }
1764	            Ok(security::EstopLevel::NetworkKill)
1765	        }
1766	        EstopLevelArg::DomainBlock => {
1767	            if domains.is_empty() {
1768	                bail!("--level domain-block requires at least one --domain");
1769	            }
1770	            if !tools.is_empty() {
1771	                bail!("--tool is not valid with --level domain-block");
1772	            }
1773	            Ok(security::EstopLevel::DomainBlock(domains))
1774	        }
1775	        EstopLevelArg::ToolFreeze => {
1776	            if tools.is_empty() {
1777	                bail!("--level tool-freeze requires at least one --tool");
1778	            }
1779	            if !domains.is_empty() {
1780	                bail!("--domain is not valid with --level tool-freeze");
1781	            }
1782	            Ok(security::EstopLevel::ToolFreeze(tools))
1783	        }
1784	    }
1785	}
1786	
1787	fn build_resume_selector(
1788	    network: bool,
1789	    domains: Vec<String>,
1790	    tools: Vec<String>,
1791	) -> Result<security::ResumeSelector> {
1792	    let selected =
1793	        usize::from(network) + usize::from(!domains.is_empty()) + usize::from(!tools.is_empty());
1794	    if selected > 1 {
1795	        bail!("Use only one of --network, --domain, or --tool for estop resume");
1796	    }
1797	    if network {
1798	        return Ok(security::ResumeSelector::Network);
1799	    }
1800	    if !domains.is_empty() {
1801	        return Ok(security::ResumeSelector::Domains(domains));
1802	    }
1803	    if !tools.is_empty() {
1804	        return Ok(security::ResumeSelector::Tools(tools));
1805	    }
1806	    Ok(security::ResumeSelector::KillAll)
1807	}

1809	fn print_estop_status(state: &security::EstopState) {
1810	    println!("Estop status:");
1811	    println!(
1812	        "  engaged:        {}",
1813	        if state.is_engaged() { "yes" } else { "no" }
1814	    );
1815	    println!(
1816	        "  kill_all:       {}",
1817	        if state.kill_all { "active" } else { "inactive" }
1818	    );
1819	    println!(
1820	        "  network_kill:   {}",
1821	        if state.network_kill {

1881	/// Gracefully shutdown a running gateway via the admin endpoint.
1882	async fn shutdown_gateway(host: &str, port: u16) -> Result<()> {
1883	    let url = format!("http://{host}:{port}/admin/shutdown");
1884	    let client = reqwest::Client::new();
1885	
1886	    match client
1887	        .post(&url)
1888	        .timeout(std::time::Duration::from_secs(5))
1889	        .send()
1890	        .await
1891	    {
1892	        Ok(response) if response.status().is_success() => Ok(()),
1893	        Ok(response) => Err(anyhow::anyhow!(
1894	            "Gateway responded with status: {}",
1895	            response.status()
1896	        )),
1897	        Err(e) => Err(anyhow::anyhow!("Failed to connect to gateway: {e}")),
1898	    }
1899	}

1901	/// Fetch the current pairing code from a running gateway.
1902	/// If `new` is true, generates a fresh pairing code via POST request.
1903	async fn fetch_paircode(host: &str, port: u16, new: bool) -> Result<Option<String>> {
1904	    let client = reqwest::Client::new();
1905	
1906	    let response = if new {
1907	        // Generate a new pairing code via POST
1908	        let url = format!("http://{host}:{port}/admin/paircode/new");
1909	        client
1910	            .post(&url)
1911	            .timeout(std::time::Duration::from_secs(5))
1912	            .send()
1913	            .await
1914	    } else {
1915	        // Get existing pairing code via GET
1916	        let url = format!("http://{host}:{port}/admin/paircode");
1917	        client
1918	            .get(&url)
1919	            .timeout(std::time::Duration::from_secs(5))
1920	            .send()
1921	            .await
1922	    };
1923	
1924	    let response = response.map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!("Failed to connect to gateway: {e}"))?;
1925	
1926	    if !response.status().is_success() {
1927	        return Err(anyhow::anyhow!(
1928	            "Gateway responded with status: {}",
1929	            response.status()
1930	        ));
1931	    }
1932	
1933	    let json: serde_json::Value = response
1934	        .json()
1935	        .await
1936	        .map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!("Failed to parse response: {e}"))?;
1937	
1938	    if json.get("success").and_then(|v| v.as_bool()) != Some(true) {
1939	        return Ok(None);
1940	    }
1941	
1942	    Ok(json
1943	        .get("pairing_code")
1944	        .and_then(|v| v.as_str())
1945	        .map(String::from))
1946	}

1985	#[cfg(unix)]
1986	fn set_owner_only_permissions(path: &std::path::Path) -> Result<()> {
1987	    use std::os::unix::fs::PermissionsExt;

1997	fn save_pending_oauth_login(config: &Config, pending: &PendingOAuthLogin) -> Result<()> {
1998	    let path = pending_oauth_login_path(config, &pending.provider);
1999	    if let Some(parent) = path.parent() {
2000	        std::fs::create_dir_all(parent)?;
2001	    }
2002	    let secret_store = pending_oauth_secret_store(config);
2003	    let encrypted_code_verifier = secret_store.encrypt(&pending.code_verifier)?;
2004	    let persisted = PendingOAuthLoginFile {
2005	        provider: Some(pending.provider.clone()),
2006	        profile: pending.profile.clone(),
2007	        code_verifier: None,
2008	        encrypted_code_verifier: Some(encrypted_code_verifier),
2009	        state: pending.state.clone(),
2010	        created_at: pending.created_at.clone(),
2011	    };
2012	    let tmp = path.with_extension(format!(
2013	        "tmp.{}.{}",
2014	        std::process::id(),
2015	        chrono::Utc::now().timestamp_nanos_opt().unwrap_or_default()
2016	    ));
2017	    let json = serde_json::to_vec_pretty(&persisted)?;
2018	    std::fs::write(&tmp, json)?;
2019	    set_owner_only_permissions(&tmp)?;
2020	    std::fs::rename(tmp, &path)?;
2021	    set_owner_only_permissions(&path)?;
2022	    Ok(())
2023	}

2025	fn load_pending_oauth_login(config: &Config, provider: &str) -> Result<Option<PendingOAuthLogin>> {
2026	    let path = pending_oauth_login_path(config, provider);
2027	    if !path.exists() {
2028	        return Ok(None);
2029	    }
2030	    let bytes = std::fs::read(&path)?;
2031	    if bytes.is_empty() {
2032	        return Ok(None);
2033	    }
2034	    let persisted: PendingOAuthLoginFile = serde_json::from_slice(&bytes)?;
2035	    let secret_store = pending_oauth_secret_store(config);
2036	    let code_verifier = if let Some(encrypted) = persisted.encrypted_code_verifier {
2037	        secret_store.decrypt(&encrypted)?
2038	    } else if let Some(plaintext) = persisted.code_verifier {
2039	        plaintext
2040	    } else {
2041	        bail!("Pending {} login is missing code verifier", provider);
2042	    };
2043	    Ok(Some(PendingOAuthLogin {
2044	        provider: persisted.provider.unwrap_or_else(|| provider.to_string()),
2045	        profile: persisted.profile,
2046	        code_verifier,
2047	        state: persisted.state,
2048	        created_at: persisted.created_at,
2049	    }))
2050	}

2055	        let _ = file.set_len(0);

2087	async fn import_openai_codex_auth_profile(
2088	    auth_service: &auth::AuthService,
2089	    profile: &str,
2090	    import_path: &std::path::Path,
2091	) -> Result<()> {
2092	    #[derive(Deserialize)]
2093	    struct CodexAuthTokens {
2094	        access_token: String,
2095	        #[serde(default)]
2096	        refresh_token: Option<String>,
2097	        #[serde(default)]
2098	        id_token: Option<String>,
2099	        #[serde(default)]
2100	        account_id: Option<String>,
2101	    }
2102	
2103	    #[derive(Deserialize)]
2104	    struct CodexAuthFile {
2105	        tokens: CodexAuthTokens,
2106	    }
2107	
2108	    let raw = std::fs::read_to_string(import_path)
2109	        .with_context(|| format!("Failed to read import file {}", import_path.display()))?;
2110	    let imported: CodexAuthFile = serde_json::from_str(&raw)
2111	        .with_context(|| format!("Failed to parse import file {}", import_path.display()))?;
2112	    let expires_at = auth::openai_oauth::extract_expiry_from_jwt(&imported.tokens.access_token);
2113	
2114	    let token_set = auth::profiles::TokenSet {
2115	        access_token: imported.tokens.access_token,
2116	        refresh_token: imported.tokens.refresh_token,
2117	        id_token: imported.tokens.id_token,
2118	        expires_at,
2119	        token_type: Some("Bearer".to_string()),
2120	        scope: None,
2121	    };
2122	
2123	    let account_id = imported
2124	        .tokens
2125	        .account_id
2126	        .or_else(|| extract_openai_account_id_for_profile(&token_set.access_token));
2127	
2128	    auth_service
2129	        .store_openai_tokens(profile, token_set, account_id, true)
2130	        .await?;
2131	
2132	    Ok(())
2133	}

2135	fn format_expiry(profile: &auth::profiles::AuthProfile) -> String {
2136	    match profile
2137	        .token_set
2138	        .as_ref()
2139	        .and_then(|token_set| token_set.expires_at)
2140	    {
2141	        Some(ts) => {
2142	            let now = chrono::Utc::now();
2143	            if ts <= now {
2144	                format!("expired at {}", ts.to_rfc3339())
2145	            } else {
2146	                let mins = (ts - now).num_minutes();
2147	                format!("expires in {mins}m ({})", ts.to_rfc3339())
2148	            }
2149	        }
2150	        None => "n/a".to_string(),
2151	    }
2152	}

2203	                            }
2204	                        }
2205	                    }
